questions.question: What is light?

options.A: A form of energy that helps us see

questions.explanation: Light is a form of energy that enables us to see objects.

questions.question: Which of the following is a natural source of light?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the main natural source of light.

questions.question: Which of the following is an artificial source of light?
questions.explanation: Electric bulbs are artificial sources of light.

questions.question: What happens when light strikes a mirror?
questions.explanation: Light bounces back from a mirror. This is called reflection.

questions.question: What type of mirror is commonly used in homes?
questions.explanation: Plane mirrors are flat mirrors used in homes.

questions.question: What is reflection of light?
questions.explanation: Reflection is the bouncing back of light from a surface.

questions.question: Which mirror forms an image of the same size as the object?
questions.explanation: Plane mirrors form images that are the same size as the object.

questions.question: Which mirror is used as a rear-view mirror in vehicles?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors provide a wider field of view.

questions.question: Which mirror curves inward?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors curve inward.

questions.question: Which mirror curves outward?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors bulge outward.

questions.question: What is the sideways reversal of images in a mirror called?
questions.explanation: Lateral inversion is the left-right reversal seen in mirrors.

questions.question: Which mirror is commonly used as a shaving mirror?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors produce magnified images when the object is close.

questions.question: Which device uses mirrors to see objects from hidden positions?
questions.explanation: Periscopes use mirrors to view objects from hidden positions.

questions.question: What type of lens is thicker in the middle?
questions.explanation: Convex lenses are thicker at the center.

questions.question: What type of lens is thinner in the middle?
questions.explanation: Concave lenses are thinner at the center.

questions.question: Which lens is used in magnifying glasses?
questions.explanation: Convex lenses enlarge objects.

questions.question: Which lens is used to correct short-sightedness?
questions.explanation: Concave lenses help correct short-sighted vision.

questions.question: Which property of light helps us see objects in mirrors?
questions.explanation: Mirrors form images due to reflection of light.

questions.question: Which mirror always forms smaller images?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors always form smaller images.

questions.question: Which mirror produces a magnified image when the object is close?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors magnify objects placed near them.

questions.question: Which object reflects light to form images?
questions.explanation: Mirrors reflect light and form images.

questions.question: What allows us to see objects around us?
questions.explanation: We see objects when light from them enters our eyes.

questions.question: Which mirror is flat in shape?
questions.explanation: Plane mirrors have flat surfaces.

questions.question: Which property of light allows images to form in mirrors?
questions.explanation: Images in mirrors form due to reflection.

questions.question: Which device uses lenses to see very small objects?
questions.explanation: Microscopes use lenses to magnify tiny objects.
